Package de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.settings

Interface SettingsProvider

All Known Implementing Classes:
CachingSettingsProvider, ColorSettingsProvider, DefaultSettingsProvider, ExtensionManager, FeatureSettingsPanel, ShortcutSettings, SlicingSettingsProvider, SMTSettingsProvider, StandardUISettings, TacletOptionsSettings, TacletTranslationOptions, TestgenOptionsPanel
public interface SettingsProvider
A settings provider is an entry in an SettingsUi.

It will be display within the settings tree by getDescription(). Tree children are determined by getChildren(). The most important functions are: applySettings(MainWindow) and getPanel(MainWindow).

Since:
2.8.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void

    The method is called if the settings should be applied to the MainWindow.

  • Method Details

    • getDescription

      String getDescription()
      A textual human readable description of the settings panel. Used at the overview tree at the left.
      Returns:
      non-null non-empty string

    • getPanel

      JPanel getPanel(MainWindow window)
      Provides the visual component for the right side.

      This panel will be wrapped inside a JScrollPane.

      You are allowed to reuse the return component. But then you should update the components, e.g. text field, during handling of the call.

      Parameters:
      window - non-null reference
      Returns:

    • getChildren

      default List<SettingsProvider> getChildren()
      Tree children of your settings dialog.

      You can use this method, if you need to split your settings into multiple panels. The children are displayed as children within the JTree.

      Returns:
      non-null list, default returns the empty list

    • applySettings

      void applySettings(MainWindow window) throws InvalidSettingsInputException
      The method is called if the settings should be applied to the MainWindow.

      If the user clicks on apply or accept, the SettingsDialog calls this method for every registered SettingsProvider.

      You should read your values from the input components and update the settings within the main window. If a field is not in the appropiate format, you should throw an InvalidSettingsInputException with the reference to the panel and component.

      Throws:
      InvalidSettingsInputException - if an input component is not properly fill. Prevent the settings dialog from closing.

    • getIcon

      @Deprecated default Icon getIcon()
      Deprecated.
      unused currently
      An icon for this settings for the JTree.
      Returns:
      nullable

    • contains

      default boolean contains(String substring)
      Determines if the given search string matches some your settings.

      Implement this function for search support. The settings dialog ask every provider if a user given search request is "handled" by a settings provider.

      Parameters:
      substring - a possible empty, non-null string
      Returns:
      true iff the search should highlight your settings provider.

    • getPriorityOfSettings

      default int getPriorityOfSettings()
      Determines the order in the JTree of the settings. Higher values are shown last.
      Returns:
